name: Hewlett-Packard's Enterprise Parallel Servers: A Graceful Transition to Scalable, High-End Performance
description: This Aberdeen Group profile evaluates Hewlett-Packard's Enterprise Parallel Server (EPS) architecture, specifically the EPS21 and EPS30 introduced May 15, 1996. The study examines EPS's combination of SMP nodes connected via a fibre channel switch for high-end OLTP and data warehousing, compares it against IBM RS/6000 SP and other competitors, documents a TPC-C benchmark of 17,826 tpmC at $396/tpmC for the EPS30, and reports positive feedback from early adopters.
